conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

First, let me read the official guidance to ensure I have the most accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now let me help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To start, could you please tell me: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello, yes I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 21 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I can see you have a son named Alex who is 21 years old and lives at home with you.

To determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask: **Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

(Child Benefit can continue for children under 20 if they stay in approved education or training, but since Alex is 21, this would affect his eligibility.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in any education or training at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me also check a few more details to be thorough.

**Is Alex currently working, and if so, for how many hours per week?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work at all right now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Just to ensure I have all the necessary information:

**Is Alex receiving any benefits in his own name, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have enough details to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete**

I'm sorry to inform you that **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why: Child Benefit is normally available for children under 16 years old. For children aged 16 or over, it can continue if they are under 20 years old AND stay in approved education or training. 

Since Alex is 21 years old, he is above the maximum age limit of 20 for Child Benefit, regardless of his circumstances. Even though he lives with you and doesn't work or claim benefits, the age limit means Child Benefit is not available for him.

If you have any other questions or if your circumstances change, please feel free to ask.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: According to Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit for a child under 16. For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. Since Alex is over 20 years old, he falls outside the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, regardless of whether he is in education or training. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old. Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for those under 20 if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, he exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, and therefore the claimant is not eligible to receive Child Benefit for him.

For children aged 16 or over, you'll normally still qualify if your child is under 20 AND they stay in approved education or training. Alex is 21 years old and is not in approved education or training. Since Alex is over 20 years old, he falls outside the age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, regardless of whether he is in education or training. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, who is 21 years old. Child Benefit is available for children under 16, or for those under 20 if they remain in approved education or training. Since Alex is 21 years old, he exceeds the maximum age limit for Child Benefit eligibility, and therefore the claimant is not eligible to receive Child Benefit for him.
